content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 12 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 209–210 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_012_page_001.jpg, DEU_012_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 151–152

---

1 These are the statutes and the ordinances which you shall be careful to do in the land which the LORD, the God of your fathers, has given to you to possess it, all the days that you live upon the ground.

2 You shall surely destroy all the places where the nations whom you are dispossessing served their gods — upon the high mountains and upon the hills and under every luxuriant tree.

3 And you shall tear down their altars and smash their pillars [masseboth], and their Asherah-poles [asherot] you shall burn with fire, and the carved images of their gods you shall hew down, and you shall destroy their name from that place.

4 You shall not do so to the LORD your God.

5 But only to the place which the LORD your God will choose from all your tribes to put his name — his dwelling [shekhinato] — there you shall seek, and there you shall come.

6 And there you shall bring your burnt-offerings and your sacrifices and your tithes [ma'aser] and the contribution of your hand and your vow-offerings and your freewill-offerings and the firstborn of your herd and of your flock.

7 And there you shall eat before the LORD your God, and you shall rejoice in all the putting-forth of your hand — you and your households — in which the LORD your God has blessed you.

8 You shall not do according to all [that] we are doing here today — every man [doing] what is right in his own eyes —

9 for you have not yet come to the rest and to the inheritance which the LORD your God is giving you.

10 And you shall cross over the Yarden and dwell in the land which the LORD your God is giving you as inheritance, and he will give you rest from all your enemies surrounding you, and you shall dwell in security.

11 And it shall be the place which the LORD your God will choose to cause his name to dwell there [leshakken shemo sham] — there you shall bring all that I am commanding you: your burnt-offerings and your sacrifices, your tithes and the contribution of your hand, and all your choice vow-offerings which you vow to the LORD.

12 And you shall rejoice before the LORD your God — you and your sons and your daughters and your male servants and your female servants and the Levite who is within your gates, for he has no portion and inheritance with you.

13 Be careful lest you offer your burnt-offerings in every place that you see.

14 But only in the place which the LORD will choose in one of your tribes — there you shall offer your burnt-offerings, and there you shall do all that I am commanding you.

15 However, in all the desire of your soul you may slaughter and eat meat according to the blessing of the LORD your God which he gives to you in all your gates; the unclean and the clean may eat of it alike, as of the gazelle and as of the deer.

16 Only the blood you shall not eat; upon the ground you shall pour it out like water.

17 You are not able to eat within your gates the tithe of your grain and of your new wine and of your fresh oil, and the firstborn of your herd and of your flock, and any of your vow-offerings which you vow, and your freewill-offerings, and the contribution of your hand.

18 But before the LORD your God you shall eat them — in the place which the LORD your God will choose — you and your son and your daughter and your male servant and your female servant and the Levite who is within your gates; and you shall rejoice before the LORD your God in all the putting-forth of your hand.

19 Be careful lest you forsake the Levite all your days upon your ground.

20 When the LORD your God enlarges your territory as he has spoken to you, and you say, "I will eat meat" — because your soul craves to eat meat — according to all the desire of your soul you may eat meat.

21 If the place which the LORD your God will choose to put his name is too far from you, then you may slaughter from your herd and from your flock which the LORD gave to you, as I commanded you, and you shall eat within your gates according to all the desire of your soul.

22 However as the gazelle and the deer are eaten, so you shall eat it; the unclean and the clean alike may eat of it.

23 Only be strong not to eat the blood, for the blood — it is the life [dam hu ha-nefesh]; and you shall not eat the life together with the meat.

24 You shall not eat it; upon the ground you shall pour it out like water.

25 You shall not eat it, in order that it may go well with you and with your sons after you, when you do what is right in the eyes of the LORD.

26 Only your holy things which you have and your vow-offerings you shall take up and come to the place which the LORD will choose.

27 And you shall make your burnt-offerings, the meat and the blood, upon the altar of the LORD your God; and the blood of your sacrifices shall be poured out against the altar of the LORD your God, and the meat you shall eat.

28 Keep and hear all these words which I am commanding you, in order that it may go well with you and with your sons after you forever, when you do the good and the right in the eyes of the LORD your God.

29 When the LORD your God cuts off the nations to whom you are going in to dispossess them from before you, and you dispossess them and dwell in their land,

30 be careful lest you be ensnared after them after their being destroyed from before you, and lest you inquire after their gods, saying, "How did these nations serve their gods? And I also will do thus."

31 You shall not do so to the LORD your God, for every abomination [to'evah] which the LORD hates they did for their gods — for also their sons and their daughters they burned in fire to their gods.

32 Every word which I am commanding you — it you shall keep to do; you shall not add upon it and you shall not diminish from it.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**V.2 — bamot (high places):** The text does not use the word בָּמוֹת (bamot) explicitly in v.2, but refers to the "high mountains and hills and under every luxuriant tree" — the typical description of Canaanite cultic sites that would come to be called bamot. The term bamot appears in other chapters. The vivid landscape-description here is preserved literally.

**V.5 — ha-maqom asher yivchar / leshakken shemo:** Hebrew הַמָּקוֹם אֲשֶׁר-יִבְחַר — "the place which he will choose." This formula (repeated seven times in this chapter) is the foundational centralization-of-worship principle. The divine name is described as "dwelling" there: לְשַׁכֵּן שְׁמוֹ שָׁם (v.11) — "to cause his name to dwell there." The verb שָׁכַן (shakhan) is the root of the theological concept of the Shekhinah (divine presence). The LXX (Vat. pp.151–152) renders ὃν ἐὰν ἐκλέξηται Κύριος ὁ θεός σου αὐτόν (whichever one the LORD your God shall choose).

**V.5 — shekhinato:** The MT here reads לְשִׁכְנוֹ — "for his dwelling" (infinitive construct of שָׁכַן used as noun). Some manuscripts read לְשַׁכֵּן שְׁמוֹ שָׁם (v.11 form). The Leningrad Codex (LC p.209) shows the form clearly. The LXX renders ἐπικληθῆναι τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ ἐκεῖ (for his name to be called there) — a "name theology" rendering.

**V.8 — "every man what is right in his own eyes":** Hebrew כָּל-הַיָּשָׁר בְּעֵינָיו — the identical phrase appears in Judges 17:6 and 21:25 as a description of anarchic religious chaos. Here it is used negatively: the current wilderness situation of dispersed worship will not continue in the land.

**V.11 — leshakken shemo sham:** Hebrew לְשַׁכֵּן שְׁמוֹ שָׁם — "to cause his name to dwell there." This is the Deuteronomic "Name theology" (Shem-theology): YHWH does not literally dwell in the Temple but his Name dwells there. This contrasts with the older Priestly theology of the Kavod (Glory) filling the tabernacle. Both theological strands are in the canon; the exact language of each is preserved.

**V.15 — "unclean and the clean alike":** Hebrew הַטָּמֵא וְהַטָּהוֹר — "the impure and the pure." For secular (non-sacrificial) meat consumption, ritual purity status is irrelevant. This is a deliberate relaxation of the rules that apply to sacrificial eating. The LXX renders ὁ ἀκάθαρτος καὶ ὁ καθαρός (the unclean and the clean) in agreement with MT.

**V.16 — "pour it out like water":** The prohibition of blood-consumption (repeated in vv.16, 23–25) is grounded in v.23: "the blood — it is the life [dam hu ha-nefesh]." The equation of blood with the nefesh (life-force/soul) is the theological basis for the blood prohibition throughout the Torah. "Pour it out like water" — the imagery makes the blood harmless by treating it like a common liquid rather than a sacred substance.

**V.23 — dam hu ha-nefesh:** Hebrew כִּי הַדָּם הוּא הַנָּפֶשׁ — "for the blood, it [is] the life." The pronoun הוּא (hu, he/it) creates an equation: blood = life. The LXX renders ὅτι τὸ αἷμα αὐτοῦ ψυχή ἐστιν (for its blood is [the] life). Hebrew nefesh here means life-force, not soul in the later Greek philosophical sense; "life" is the appropriate rendering.

**V.31 — child sacrifice:** The verse explicitly states that the nations burned "their sons and their daughters in fire to their gods" — הַגָּם אֶת-בְּנֵיהֶם וְאֶת-בְּנֹתֵיהֶם יִשְׂרְפוּ בָאֵשׁ לֵאלֹהֵיהֶם. The LXX confirms the reading: καὶ τοὺς υἱοὺς αὐτῶν καὶ τὰς θυγατέρας αὐτῶν κατακαίουσιν ἐν πυρί. This is presented as the defining characteristic of pagan practice that makes it to'evah (abomination) in YHWH's sight.

**V.32 — "you shall not add...you shall not diminish":** This canonical close formula (לֹא תֹסֵף עָלָיו וְלֹא תִגְרַע מִמֶּנּוּ) appears also in Deut 4:2. It marks the text as complete and inviolable. The LXX renders οὐ προσθήσεις ἐπ᾽ αὐτό, οὐδὲ ἀφελεῖς ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ (you shall not add to it, nor remove from it). The chapter ends on this closed-canon note.

**Source note:** The Vaticanus images for this chapter (Vat.gr.1209 pp.151–152) are found across two pages. Page 151 (Vat. pa_0155) contains the chapter heading CAP. VIII (the LXX numbering sometimes diverges from MT chapter numbering at this point) and continues through v.20+. Page 152 (Vat. pa_0156, which shows the heading P CAP. IX at the top, indicating beginning of LXX chapter 9 = MT Deut. 12 continuation or 13) confirms the close of MT ch.12 material. The LC folios 209–210 show the Hebrew text across two pages.

— EMET
